zh.Bucklow, Cheshire, England: 英國爾巴可洛福 希爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert MASSEY於大約1251年出生在英國爾鄧翰 梅西時，他的父親Sir William de Massey of Tatton27歲，母親Margery Mainwaring17歲。他和Alice Davenport至少有11個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1298年在英國爾享年48歲。

zh-hans.Bucklow, Cheshire, England: 英国爾巴可洛福 希爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert MASSEY于大约1251年出生在英国爾鄧翰 梅西时，他的父亲Sir William de Massey of Tatton27岁，母亲Margery Mainwaring17岁。他和Alice Davenport至少有11个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1298年在英国爾享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: MASSEY
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert MASSEY was born about 1251, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir William de Massey of Tatton, was 27 and his mother, Margery Mainwaring, was 17. He had at least 11 sons and 2 daughters with Alice Davenport. He died in 1298, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert MASSEY est né environ 1251, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Massey of Tatton, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margery Mainwaring, avait 17 ans. Il a eu au moins 11 fils et 2 filles avec Alice Davenport. Il est décédé en 1298, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert MASSEY um 1251 in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William de Massey of Tatton 27 und seine Mutter Margery Mainwaring 17. Er hatte mit Alice Davenport mindestens 11 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1298 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert MASSEY è nato il circa 1251 nel Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir William de Massey of Tatton, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Margery Mainwaring, aveva 17 anni. Lui avuto almeno 11 figli e 2 figlie con Alice Davenport. Morì nel 1298, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert MASSEYが約1251年にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで生まれた時，父Sir William de Massey of Tattonは27歳，母Margery Mainwaringは17歳であった。彼はAlice Davenportとの間に少なくとも11人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1298年にイギリスチェシュアイアで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert MASSEY(이)가 대략 1251년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de Massey of Tatton(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Mainwaring(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Alice Davenport(와)과 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1298년 영국 체실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert MASSEY nasceu aproximadamente 1251, em Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Massey of Tatton, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Margery Mainwaring, tinha 17 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 11 filhos e 2 filhas com Alice Davenport. Ele faleceu em 1298, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert MASSEY родился около 1251 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William de Massey of Tatton было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Mainwaring, 17. У него было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 2 дочери от Alice Davenport. Он умер в 1298 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert MASSEY nació aproximadamente 1251, en Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Massey of Tatton, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Margery Mainwaring, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 11 hijos y 2 hijas con Alice Davenport. Murió en 1298, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años.
